title: potentilla rupestris subsp. corsica
intro: Potentilla rupestris subsp. corsica, also known as Corsican cinquefoil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family, characterized by its hairy stems and yellow flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Potentilla rupestris subsp. corsica include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
